info.title: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM - Talent Person Profiles/Accomplishment Sections/Accomplishment Items/Large Object (LOB) Attributes - Description
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM.

tags: Talent Person Profiles/Accomplishment Sections/Accomplishment Items/Large Object (LOB) Attributes - Description

parameters.name: talentPersonProfilesUniqID
parameters.description: This is the hash key of the attributes which make up the composite key for the Talent Person Profiles resource and used to uniquely identify an instance of Talent Person Profiles. The client should not generate the hash key value. Instead, the client should query on the Talent Person Profiles collection resource in order to navigate to a specific instance of Talent Person Profiles to get the hash key.

parameters.name: ProfileSectionId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

schema.title: Profile Section ID
schema.description: Unique identifier for the technical post detail section associated with the profile.

parameters.name: AccomplishmentId
parameters.description: The value of this parameter could be a hash of the key that is used to uniquely identify the resource item. The client should not generate the hash key value.  Instead, the client should query on the collection resource with a filter to navigate to a specific resource item. For example: products?q=InventoryItemId=<value1>

default.description: The following table describes the default response for this task.
